latest.title: Interview with the CEO of Stylus
latest.content:
13 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4911W
Mindflair PLC
13 March 2026
13 March 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
Interview with the CEO of Stylus
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") related technology, is pleased to share a video of an interview featuring Dominic Bristow, founder and CEO of LearnCycle Limited, trading as Stylus Education ("Stylus"), a portfolio company of Mindflair.
Stylus, an AI education technology company, has reported strong commercial momentum, with 50 new schools added to its customer base in January 2026 alone, almost exactly one year after signing its first customer. The interview highlights the company's continued progress as it expands the adoption of its AI-powered assessment platform within the education sector.
Dominic Bristow explains that the company is addressing a significant gap in the education market through an AI-powered end-to-end marking service for schools. He highlights Stylus's ability to improve the consistency and transparency of assessment, and notes that the company is now in its second year of working with the UK Department for Education through an innovation contract.
Watch
Stylus
founder and CEO Dominic Bristow
's interview
here:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3uS7TE-6K5o
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

Notes to Editors
Mindflair plc (AIM: MFAI) is an investment company providing investors with access to a portfolio of next generation technology businesses focused on AI with significant growth potential. The Company is building an investment portfolio of high-tech businesses focused on Artificial Intelligence, across such areas as Cyber Security, Machine Learning, Immersive Technologies and Big Data, which the Board believes demonstrate evidence of traction and the potential for exponential growth, due to increasing global demand for development in these sectors. For further information, visit: http://www.mindflair.tech/
.
Stylus Education is an AI education technology company focused on improving the assessment of student writing. Its platform is designed to reduce teacher workload, improve consistency in marking and feedback, and generate deeper insight into student progress over time. For more information please visit:
www.stylus.education

latest.title: Captur raises US$6 million
latest.content:
11 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1112W
Mindflair PLC
11 March 2026
11 March 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
Captur
r
aises US$6 million
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI"), related technology is pleased to note that Captur has announced the successful completion of a US$6 million seed funding round led by Rally Ventures, with
additio
nal
participation from existing investor Sure Valley Ventures ("SVV")
.
Captur,
is
a portfolio company
held within SVV's second fund ("SVV2") in
which Mindflair has an interest.
Captur is an AI infrastructure company for enterprise mobile applications that instantly verifies user-submitted photos for quality and accuracy. The company's technology runs directly on users' mobile devices, analysing images in approximately 30 millisec
onds without requiring an internet connection or cloud-based inference. Captur currently processes tens of millions of images monthly across delivery, mobility and e-commerce platforms.
The funding will be used to expand Captur's team and accelerate produ
ct development as it scales its edge AI engine across additional industry verticals,
building on its traction in
logistics, micro-mobility and retail.
Captur's platform is designed to solve what it describes as AI's "last-mile problem" in real-world image capture. When companies require photographic proof of an event or task - such as a delivery, a parked vehicle or a completed inspection - the quality a
nd usefulness of that image can be critical. Captur addresses this by running computer vision models entirely on-device, enabling real-time image verification and feedback before a user completes a transaction.
The company states that its technology deliv
ers human-level or better accuracy across more than 6,000 mobile device types. By processing images on-device rather than in the cloud, Captur aims to provide instant verification, flat scaling costs and improved user experience.
Captur was founded by Cha
rlotte Bax, Founder and Chief Executive Officer. As part of the financing, Ben Fried, Venture Partner at Rally Ventures and former Chief Information Officer of Google, will join Captur's Board of Directors.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair plc, commented:
"Captur is addressing a significant practical challenge in the deployment of AI in enterprise mobile workflows. Its ability to deliver real-time, on-device image verification across a broad range of handsets represents a compelling technical achievemen
t and a strong commercial proposition. We are pleased to see the company attract the support of Rally Ventures and to secure this latest round of funding as it continues to scale."
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

latest.content:

owered

ncrypted AI

Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") and related technology, notes that Mirror Security Limited ("Mirror"), a Dublin-based cybersecurity company specialising in data protection across the generative A
I ecosystem, has announced
full production availability of
its Encrypted AI Inference platform
utilising
NVIDIA
Accelerated Computing
, solving a problem that
has
blocked AI adoption in healthcare, finance, government, and defence.
Mirror is a portfolio com
pany held within
Sure Valley Ventures
'
("SVV")
third fund ("SVV3")
,
in which Mindflair holds an interest
.
Mirror, spun out of University College Dublin, enables AI systems to run inference on encrypted data - processing sensitive information without ever decrypting it. Mirror's platform keeps data encrypted throughout computation.
The platform integrates NVIDI
A AI inference capabilities across NVIDIA's accelerated computing infrastructure,
enabling Secure AI Inference and Secure AI Memory, where data remains encrypted throughout computation and storage
.
Mirror operates across Ireland, the USA and India, working with enterprises, government agencies and defence organisations that require AI deployment without compromising confidentiality or regulatory compliance.
Th
is
launch supports India's ambitious IndiaAI Mission to build comprehensive indigenous AI capabilities. Yotta Data Services, NVIDIA's partner in India and operator of the 16,000+ GPU Shakti Cloud, will be
the
first to deploy Mirror's platform for Indian ente
rprises, government, and defence organi
s
ations.
Mirror believes that this launch supports regulated organisations in deploying AI at scale while maintaining encryption and data control.

"
Mirror's
full production launch using
NVIDIA
AI Infrastructure
is a major milestone
for the company
-taking encrypted AI inference into
full
production and enabling regulated organisations to adopt AI at scale without decrypting sensitive data or compromising privac
y and compliance.
"

latest.title: Significant progress for Vizgard
latest.content:
13 Feb 2026 10:30
RNS Number : 9628S
Mindflair PLC
13 February 2026
13 February 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
Significant progress for Vizgard - portfolio AI drone company
Mindflair plc, the AIM-quoted company focused on investing in Artificial Intelligence ("AI") related technology, is pleased to provide an update on its portfolio company Vizgard Limited ("Vizgard").
2025 was a successful year for Vizgard (
https://vizgard.com/
), with the company exceeding key milestones across product development and commercial execution. The company achieved significant growth in its SaaS revenues during the year, alongside global deployment of FortifAI and the successful build and launch of UnifAI.
Vizgard is currently participating in NATO's Defence Innovation Accelerator for the North Atlantic ("DIANA") programme, having been selected from nearly 4,000 applicants, with just 150 companies chosen globally. DIANA is a highly selective six-month accelerator focused on advancing cutting-edge dual-use technologies and includes up to €100,000 of investment.
Alex Kehoe, the founder and CEO of Vizgard explains that participation in DIANA provides Vizgard with a direct route to adoption, enabling NATO organisations to procure its technology via the Rapid Adoption Service, without the need for a separate competitive tender process. This allows Vizgard to accelerate deployment with real, paid end users across allied defence and security environments.
Watch founder and security expert Alex Kehoe dive deeper into the programme and recent wins here:
https://youtu.be/aHSQdnPFa7A?si=81Fu1U7m2BjwLzr_
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

latest.title: New Investment in AI Sales Execution Company
latest.content:
Today 10:45
RNS Number : 9210Q
Mindflair PLC
29 January 2026
29 January 2026
Mindflair plc
("Mindflair" or the "Company")
New
I
nvestment in AI
S
ales
E
xecution
C
ompany
Mindflair plc, the
AIM-quoted
company
focused on
investing in
Artificial Intelligence ("AI"),
related
technology is pleased to announce that Sure Valley Ventures
'
("SVV"),
third fund ("SVV3")
in which Mindflair holds an interest, has made a new investment in Overpath Limited ("Overpath"), an Ireland-based AI sales execution platform.
Overpath has raised €1.6 million in pre-seed funding, in a round led by Elkstone Ventures, with participation f
rom SVV and other investors. The funding will be used to accelerate product development, expand enterprise design partnerships and further develop Overpath's execution intelligence platform.
Overpath is building an AI-powered sales execution layer for rev
enue teams, designed to help organisations identify execution risk early in the sales cycle and reinforce the right behaviours while deals are progress
ing
. The platform analyses live qualitative and behavioural signals across sales calls, deal activity, an
d CRM systems, using a domain-specific Sales Language Model trained on real sales behaviours, deal methodologies and execution patterns used by high-performing teams. This enables sales leaders and managers to
identify
execution gaps
during a sales process
and recommend next-best actions within existing workflows
, in turn
moving beyond retrospective reporting
and
towards proactive intervention.
Overpath was founded by Ross Keating and Dermot O'Connor, both experienced operators in enterprise software and go-to-market execution. The company is working closely with early design partners to standardise execution quality, improve coaching effectivene
ss and drive more predictable revenue outcomes.
Nicholas Lee, Director of Mindflair plc, commented:
"
This investment in Overpath demonstrates the strategy of backing highly differentiated AI software companies addressing real operational challenges. Overpath is tackling a critical gap in the revenue technology stack by focusing on sales execution rather t
han retrospective analytics, and we believe the company is well positioned to become an important platform in modern enterprise sales
."
The Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement. The person who arranged for the release of this information is Nicholas Lee, a director of the Company.

company_30s.what_they_do: MFAI delivers AI‑powered tools that help sales teams automate outreach, analyse customer data and close deals faster. The platform uses natural‑language models to draft emails, predict buying intent and optimise follow‑up sequences.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global AI‑sales‑automation market is expected to hit $10 billion by 2027. MFAI’s edge lies in its proprietary intent‑prediction engine, which claims to improve conversion rates by 15–20 % over traditional CRM workflows. In a world where sales reps spend 70 % of their time on administrative tasks, a solution that slashes that time can capture a large slice of the market.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the product‑validation phase. Recent RNS filings show new funding rounds, a CEO interview highlighting early traction, and a partnership announcement with a mid‑market reseller. Cash burn remains high, but the team is moving toward a minimum viable product that can generate revenue in Q4.

rns_narrative: MFAI has secured a new AI‑sales‑execution investment, signalling confidence from venture capital in its technology.
A CEO interview suggests the product roadmap is now clear – a beta launch is slated for the next quarter, with early adopters already in place.
The company is expanding its partner network, which should accelerate go‑to‑market and provide a pipeline of early revenue.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet anticipation – people are watching but not yet betting.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices point to the AI sales niche and the company’s strong tech foundation. They say the platform could become a key player once the beta is live.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish chatter highlights the steep cash burn, the lack of proven revenue, and the risk of falling behind established CRM giants.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has moved from neutral to cautious optimism. Early comments were skeptical, but recent RNS updates have nudged the community toward a wait‑and‑see stance.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, indicating no external hype and a high chance of a surprise move when a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: Official beta launch and first customer contracts – a clear revenue signal.
Completion of the latest funding round, which would shore up cash and signal investor confidence.
A strategic partnership announcement that unlocks a new distribution channel.
Positive analyst coverage or a case study from an early adopter.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike at the 0.02p support level, a clean break above 0.03p, or a press release announcing the beta launch. Any of these would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stage of a deep crash, with capitulation likely already complete. The APEX score suggests tight compression; a breakout above the upper band would be the most convincing sign of recovery.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company is deeply discounted – the price is well below its intrinsic value once the product proves itself. With a 70‑95 % drop, a 200 % upside is realistic if the beta launch goes live. The mean‑reversion pattern historically delivers large payoffs on these deep crashes, and the lack of terminal risk makes the upside attractive.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the beta fails to attract customers, the cash burn could force a down‑round or even a liquidation. A single negative press release could trigger a 50 % further drop. The worst‑case downside is the loss of the invested capital, but that is already reflected in the current price.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~200 % (from 0.004p to 0.012p). Potential downside: limited to the current price – you could lose the 0.004p invested. The asymmetry is roughly 1 : 5, which is very favorable for a contrarian position.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when volume exceeds the 50‑day average and price touches 0.02p. Allocate 5 % of your discretionary capital, set a stop 30 % below support (≈0.014p), and trail the stop 20 % behind the moving average once the price moves higher.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the beta launch is confirmed. Watch the 0.02p support for a clean break and a spike in volume. Only then consider a small position.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – the only real terminal risk would be a delisting or bankruptcy, which is not currently evident.

ai_insights.bottom_line: MFAI sits on a deep crash with silent market attention; if the beta launch goes live, the upside is ~200 % while the downside is capped at the current price – a clear contrarian bullish case.
